message:
My mom has THREE escape route maps taped inside our kitchen cabinets. Three. I used to laugh about it—until last month when I actually *needed* to grab one during a small grease fire to show my kids where to go. Turns out, being the person who thinks through "what if" scenarios isn't annoying; it's kind of the opposite.

I've started doing the same thing now—nothing fancy, just a simple route marked on a paper tucked in an easy-to-find spot. And honestly? Checking that the smoke alarms actually work (yes, I push the button) takes like two minutes and feels weirdly good.

Do you have an escape plan written down, or is it mostly in your head right now?
